title: 10 best herbal tinctures for enlarged spleen
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for enlarged spleen are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have been traditionally used to support spleen function and reduce its enlargement. These tinctures help by stimulating the spleen's natural filtering process, promoting healthy circulation, and reducing inflammation. Examples of herbal tinctures that may be beneficial for enlarged spleen include Turmeric, Ginger, and Dandelion root, which can improve overall health and quality of life. By using these tinctures, individuals with enlarged spleens may experience reduced pain, improved digestion, and enhanced energy levels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because its unique blend of compounds, particularly adiantum, has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote liver detoxification. The tincture's antioxidant properties help to soothe the liver and kidneys, which in turn can alleviate pressure on the spleen, reducing its enlargement. Additionally, Maidenhair tree tinctures may also help to improve blood flow and circulation, further contributing to a healthy spleen function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to spleen enlargement by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen.

Immunomodulatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help regulate inflammation and promote a balanced immune response, potentially reducing symptoms of an enlarged spleen.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it a valuable part for treating an enlarged spleen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees, ensuring they are free of pesticides and heavy metals.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the washed ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter, then blend them with 50% vodka at a ratio of 1:2 for 10 minutes.
Steep the leaf-vodka mixture in a glass jar with a lid at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with the condition. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to inhibit inflammation-causing enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the liver and spleen from oxidative damage, promoting overall health and well-being. As a natural and effective remedy, turmeric tinctures can provide relief and support for individuals struggling with an enlarged spleen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the spleen, which can alleviate discomfort and symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the spleen from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and enlargement of the organ.
Immunomodulatory property: Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response and reduce excessive inflammation in the spleen, thereby helping to shrink an enlarged spleen.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various liver and spleen-related disorders, making them a valuable part for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried turmeric powder also known as curcuma longa and pure vodka at a ratio of one part powder to four parts vodka.
Combine one teaspoon of the turmeric powder mixture with 4 ounces of vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing it to macerate and steep.
After four weeks shake the mixture well then filter it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper top at a dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs allium sativum at dawn when dew is still present on leaves.
Cut the garlic into thin slices and weigh out 20 grams of sliced garlic for each liter of menstruum.
Combine the sliced garlic with vodka or grain alcohol as a menstruum in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids then store the tincture in an amber glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of fresh ginger root and chop it into small pieces.
Combine chopped ginger with 60% proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark cool place for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 days.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh peppermint leaves in the early morning after dew has dried.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because of its unique ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The saponins present in burdock root have been shown to promote circulation and alleviate congestion, which can help to shrink an enlarged spleen caused by conditions such as cirrhosis or infection. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the surrounding tissues and reduce swelling, promoting a return to normal spleen function and alleviating symptoms of discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and lignans, which can help reduce inflammation in the spleen and surrounding tissues, alleviating discomfort and swelling associated with an enlarged spleen.

Antioxidant property: Burdock tincture is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to spleen enlargement and other chronic diseases.

Blood purification property: Burdock root has been traditionally used to cleanse the blood of toxins, impurities, and heavy metals, which can accumulate in the spleen and cause its enlargement. By purifying the blood, burdock tincture may help reduce the strain on the spleen and promote its healthy functioning.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots have a long history of use in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and swelling, making them effective for alleviating symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the burdock plant is rich in saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce discomfort and improve digestion associated with an enlarged spleen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots in the morning when dew is still present on leaves.
Cleanly chop the roots into small pieces and rinse them thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine 200g of chopped roots with 700ml of high-proof vodka and allow it to macerate for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use allowing the flavors to meld together.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat enlarged spleen with herbal tinctures is by combining Burdock root, Turmeric, and Ginger to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Echinacea and Garlic have antimicrobial properties that help fight off infections, while Dandelion root supports liver function and promotes detoxification. Take 10-15 drops of each tincture three times a day for several weeks under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to enlarged spleen that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions affecting the liver, gallbladder, and digestive system. For instance, herbal tinctures like Milk Thistle and Dandelion root have been traditionally used to treat liver congestion and inflammation. Artichoke leaf extract has also shown promise in treating gallstones and bile duct issues. Additionally, herbs like Turmeric and Ginger may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders. These tinctures can be used individually or in combination to promote overall gut health and well-being.
